acceptance_conditions.condition: contract, protocol, generated routes, denominators, and all source hashes remain exact
three groups produce 24 rows and 72 executions with three same-seed arms
all six frozen routes retain semantic fidelity at least 0.9999999999
portfolio generated-automatic mean is at least -0.005 and bootstrap lower at least -0.01
portfolio generated-denominator mean is at least -0.005 and bootstrap lower at least -0.015
all three backend groups have mean generated-denominator delta at least -0.02
zero rows have generated-denominator regression below -0.05
each backend mean is at least -0.02
challenge commitment, hidden seeds, row hashes, reveal, and transcript replay
hardware, temporal, real-device transfer, general generation, advantage, BQP, solved-frontier, and credit claims remain false

challenge_protocol.automatic_rule: fresh optimization-level-3 compile with a hidden transpiler seed
challenge_protocol.denominator_rule: replay the frozen minimum-calibration-exposure route from 80 independent optimization-level-3 transpiler seeds
challenge_protocol.generated_rule: recompile each R150 selected mapping, policy, and realization seed
challenge_protocol.shared_seed_rule: all three arms in one row share one simulator seed

claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported_even_if_accepted: temporal transfer, real-device transfer, hardware performance, general route-generation advantage, quantum advantage, BQP separation, or solved B4/B8/B10

phase_protocol: commit a fresh secret after public preregistration
derive hidden transpiler, simulator, and bootstrap seeds
replay generated, frozen strong-denominator, and fresh automatic arms
reveal and verify the acceptance transcript
research_question: Can the frozen R149 generation recipe remain noninferior to strong seeded denominators on three previously unused seven-qubit fake backends?
